elements.sent-2411-001: Abstract~~~ The small mass limit of the Langevin equation perturbed by $\be$-stable L\'{e vy noise is considered by rewriting it in the form of slow-fast system, and spliting the fast component into three parts.}
elements.sent-2411-002: By exploring the three parts respectively, the limit equation and the convergence rate are derived.
elements.sent-2411-003: Keywords~~~{Langevin equation , {Smoluchowski--Kramers approximation , {Averaging , {Singular pertubation 2020 Mathematics Subject Classification~~~60G51, 60H10.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-004: Smoluchowski--Kramers (SK for short) approximation is initially proposed by Smoluchowski\cite{SMOL16 and Kramers\cite{KRAM40 to derive an effective approximation to a Langevin equation which describes the motion of a particle with small mass.}}
elements.sent-2411-005: Roughly speaking, the equation \begin{equation}\ep \ddot{u^\ep}+\dot{u^\ep}=b(u^\ep)+\sigma(u^\ep)\dot{W}\end{equation} is approximated, as $\ep\rightarrow 0$, in some sense by the equation \begin{equation}\dot{u}=b(u)+\sigma(u)\dot{W}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-006: Formally, the limit equation is obtained by dropping the term $\ep \ddot{u}$\,.
elements.sent-2411-007: There is fruitful work on SK approximation for Langevin equations with Gaussian white noise\cite[e.g.]{CX22, FH11, FH13, SW21, SW22, SWW24,LJW24 .}
elements.sent-2411-008: The case that $W$ is an infinite dimensional Brownian motion is firstly studied by Cerrai and Freidlin\cite{CF06A, CF06B .}
elements.sent-2411-009: There is also some work concerned with SK approximation with colored noise which is highly oscillating in time \cite{HOTT13,HMV15 or with L\'{e vy noise \cite{ZHAN08,chaos24 .}}}
elements.sent-2411-010: In this paper, we consider the following Langevin equation driven by a stable L\'{e vy process \begin{equation}\begin{cases} \ep\ddot{\ue}(t)+\due(t)=f(\ue(t))+\ep^\al\dot{L}(t), \ue(0)=u_0, \quad \due(0)=v_0. \end{cases}\label{wave}\end{equation} Here, $0\leq\al<1$ is a constant, and $L$ is a $\be$-stable process whose properties are detailed in Section~\ref{preli .}}
elements.sent-2411-011: System (\ref{wave ) describes the motion of a particle with mass $\ep$ in an athermal fluctuation environment.}
elements.sent-2411-012: Equation (\ref{wave ) can also be seen as a singularly perturbed differential equation with a random noise, which has attracted many researchers' interest~\cite[e.g.]{LW09, LRW11 .}}
elements.sent-2411-013: One of the difficulty lies on the fact that, unlike Brownian motions or L\'{e vy processes without big jump, $\be$-stable processes do not have finite second order moment.}
elements.sent-2411-014: Another difficulty is that the L\'{e vy measure of a $\be$-stable process is infinite.}
elements.sent-2411-015: Formally, the effective approximation model of (\ref{wave ) can also be obtained by dropping the $\ep \ddot{\ue}$ term, that is, \begin{equation}\dot{\bar{u}}^\ep(t)=f(\bue(t))+\ep^\al\dot{L}(t),\quad \bue(0)=u_0. \label{aveq}\end{equation} Obviously, the statement above reduces to the classical SK approximation in the case $\al=0$.}
elements.sent-2411-016: Here we introduce a splitting technique of the solution \cite{LRW11 to show the approximation rigorously.}
